text.title: GCP Landing Zone Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gcp-landing-zone methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Use a folder-per-environment pattern (Production / Staging / Development) under the organisation root plus a Shared Infrastructure folder for host projects.
rule.rationale: Folder boundaries map cleanly to IAM scope and SCP-style org policies; ad-hoc layouts force per-project re-binding.

rule.statement: Disable service-account key creation at org level via constraints/iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation and adopt Workload Identity Federation everywhere.
rule.rationale: Org-level disablement is the only durable gate; per-project enforcement leaks through new projects.

rule.statement: Forbid primitive roles (Owner / Editor / Viewer) at project or org level via deny policies; require predefined or custom roles only.
rule.rationale: Primitive roles bypass least-privilege and cannot be audited at scale.

rule.statement: Require MFA and corporate SSO for all human identities before granting production access.
rule.rationale: Without SSO/MFA, identity becomes the breach vector regardless of cloud-side hardening.

rule.statement: Aggregate audit, data-access and VPC-flow logs into a dedicated log-sink project, and export billing into BigQuery from day one.
rule.rationale: Day-one logging and billing exports are non-replayable; bolting them on later loses the early history.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
